message:
feat(profile_sweep): emit per-screen metrics in JSON summary

profile_sweep previously aggregated frame timings, alloc counts,
and pipeline byte/cell/present samples into a single global
distribution per render mode. That hid which screen was responsible
for the tail latency or alloc spikes — so a regression in one
screen showed up only as a small bump in the global p99 and was
easy to miss in CI diffs.

Add per-screen tracking that piggybacks on the existing inner
loop without changing the outer aggregates:

- New ScreenMetrics struct holds per-screen Vec<u64> for frame_us,
  allocs, alloc_bytes, and (in pipeline mode) bytes / changed_cells
  / present_us. Pre-allocates capacity = cycles * sizes per screen
  so the hot loop never reallocates.

- screen_metrics is built once at sweep start, indexed parallel
  to screens::screen_ids().

- Inside the inner loop, the existing branch that runs render_full
  / render_diff / pipeline.render now first writes its results
  into local bytes_emitted / changed_cells / present_us, and after
  the alloc-region snapshot is taken, calls
  screen_metrics[screen_idx].record_common(...) and
  .record_pipeline(...) for the pipeline mode. The global
  per_frame_* vectors still get the same samples, so existing
  global summary output is unchanged.

- Two small helpers — sorted_copy() and metric_summary_json() —
  factor out the sort+percentile pattern used by both the global
  block and the per-screen block, so each metric's
  p50/p95/p99/max object is built consistently.

- ScreenMetrics::to_json(render_mode) produces:
    {
      screen, title, index, frames,
      frame_time_us: { p50, p95, p99, max },
      allocations_per_frame: { ... },
      allocated_bytes_per_frame: { ... },
      pipeline: null | {
        changed_cells_per_frame: { ... },
        present_us: { ... },
        bytes_emitted: { ... }
      }
    }
  pipeline is null in non-pipeline render modes.

- The JSON summary gains a top-level "screens": [...] array
  (existing keys unchanged), so tooling can diff per-screen
  regressions without re-parsing the raw frame log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
